prs.title: Revise README for VectoSpace project details
prs.body: Updated project name, features, architecture, and installation instructions in README.

prs.title: feat : Diagnosis Engine (Prompt  / Diagnostician)
prs.body:
Adds a complete Diagnosis Engine for VectoSpace that analyzes student performance against goals and generates structured recommendations using LLM + rule-based fallback.
Introduces prompt engineering with strict JSON output, guardrails, and few-shot examples to prevent hallucination.
Implements a typed diagnosis pipeline with validation, plus a redesigned Streamlit UI for interactive insights and reports.
Updates documentation with architecture, schema, and usage details.

In Shiki v3, comment-prefixed highlight markers such as:

### Description

This PR fixes an issue in **bash/shell code blocks** where syntax highlighting behaved incorrectly when text was enclosed within angle brackets.

**Example:**
```bash
pfinfo <usual_guide>
```

### Linked Issue

#1020

prs.title: Added new sort_algo
prs.body:
A clean and efficient Python implementation of the QuickSort algorithm, utilizing recursive partitioning to sort arrays with an average time complexity of O(n log n) and space complexity of O(log n).

This project demonstrates the divide-and-conquer approach used in sorting algorithms, with well-structured code, test cases, and performance benchmarks for analysis and comparison.

🚀 Features
- Recursive QuickSort implementation
- Average O(n log n) time complexity
- Optimized space usage (O(log n))
- Includes unit tests and benchmark results
- Simple and readable Python code structure

prs.title: Tower Defence Game
prs.body: Tower Defense Game - A classic strategy game built with Python and Pygame. Strategically place three types of towers (Basic, Sniper, Cannon) to defend against increasingly difficult waves of enemies. Manage your resources, earn money from kills, and survive as long as possible. Features real-time combat, progressive difficulty, and visual effects.

prs.title: added rat_in_maze algo
prs.body:
🐀 Rat in a Maze – Backtracking Algorithm

This project implements the classic Rat in a Maze problem using Recursion and Backtracking. The goal is to find all possible paths for a rat to move from the top-left corner to the bottom-right corner of a maze, represented as a binary matrix (1 → open cell, 0 → blocked cell).

The algorithm explores all four directions — Down, Left, Right, and Up, ensuring no cell is visited twice in a single path. It demonstrates core DSA concepts such as DFS traversal, backtracking, and path generation.

Features:

- Uses Depth-First Search (DFS) with backtracking.
- Finds all valid paths from source to destination.
- Supports any N x N maze input.
- Returns sorted list of path strings for consistency.

prs.title: made space_shooter_game
prs.body: Add Space Shooter Game: - Implemented a classic arcade-style space shooter using HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript - Added smooth keyboard controls (← → for movement, Spacebar to shoot) - Integrated dynamic asteroid spawning with randomized speed and difficulty progression - Developed scoring (+10 per asteroid) and lives system (3 lives to start) - Added visual effects including particle explosions, starry background, and glowing UI - Built a Game Over screen with final score display and restart functionality - Organized project into index.html, style.css, script.js, and README.md for clean structure

prs.title: add Login activity
prs.body: Added a Sign In and Sign Up page to the project to enable user authentication interface. The pages are built using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript and feature a modern, responsive design with intuitive input forms, buttons, and user feedback elements. Key Features: - Sign Up page with fields for username, email, and password - Sign In page with email and password fields - Form validation for required inputs and proper email format - Modern, responsive, and mobile-friendly UI - Lightweight and framework-free - Why this change: - Provides a basic authentication interface for user - Improves the overall functionality and completeness of the application - Enhances user experience with clear feedback and easy navigation

prs.title: Added my Task_planner mini project
prs.body: A simple and functional Todo List application built with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Add, complete, delete, and filter tasks with a modern, responsive design and smooth animations. Perfect for staying organized and tracking your daily tasks.

prs.title: Added percentage Love
prs.body: Fixed the issue where the percentage was not visible in the application. Updated the UI so that the percentage is now displayed correctly and consistently across views. This ensures users can clearly track their progress without confusion. Testing was done on multiple screens to confirm visibility, proper alignment, and readability

prs.title: Update LICENSE with MIT license and add SPDX identifier
prs.body:
Changes made:

- Replaced/updated the existing LICENSE file with the full MIT License text.
- Added SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT to support automated license detection.
- Updated copyright notice to (c) 2025 CipherYuvraj.

Why this matters:

- Ensures the project is officially recognized as MIT-licensed.
- Makes it easier for developers, contributors, and tools to understand and respect licensing terms.
- Strengthens the project’s open-source credibility and usability.

prs.title: My artwork
prs.body: This piece features a glowing, animated sun set against a vibrant gradient sky that transitions from warm oranges and yellows to deep purples. Three layered mountain silhouettes create depth, while twinkling stars and flying birds add life to the scene. Subtle wave effects at the base complete the tranquil composition.
